insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Sweden's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team gaining momentum in half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight increase in their overall performance. However, when Sweden was pressing before a hydration break, they actually lost momentum, with their performance declining by an average of 0.100 minutes, and they were unable to regain it after the break. On the other hand, when Sweden wasn't pressing before a break, they were able to capitalize on the pause, coming back stronger with an average increase of 0.300 minutes, suggesting that the team may benefit from a more conservative approach leading up to hydration breaks. This data implies that Sweden's strategy going into hydration breaks may significantly impact their overall match performance.
